tasks.title: Create a clean implementation plan for a more dynamic and colourful weather/time widget
tasks.description: The current widget in the bottom left of Mission Control works well, but it could be more appealing. Different fonts, sizing, and perhaps a dynamic colour system that adapts to either weather or the time of the day, anything is possible. Create a plan to give it more of a wow-factor, while still being kinda elegant/minimalistic.

run.summary: Here's the result summary: Feedback addressed:** **Sans-serif temperature:** Temperature font changed from Georgia serif to `var(--font-sans)` (Avenir Next), 15px / 500 weight — balanced with the 13px nav items **Comp…
run.feedbackNote: Everything looks good, especially the dynamic time/colour concept,  but change the temperature from the big serif font to a cleaner sans-serif font, that's more in balance with the other elements. Additionally, it does not need to fill the entire width of the left menu bar, it can be slimmer (or even circular) as well, as long as that complements the design.

feedback.note: Everything looks good, especially the dynamic time/colour concept,  but change the temperature from the big serif font to a cleaner sans-serif font, that's more in balance with the other elements. Additionally, it does not need to fill the entire width of the left menu bar, it can be slimmer (or even circular) as well, as long as that complements the design.

tasks.title: Create a clean implementation plan for a more dynamic and collapsible left menu bar
tasks.description: The current left menu bar in Mission Control works well, but it is a bit static. Ideally we want it to be collapsible, so we can create more screen space. When collapsed, it should only show the icons (these are up for improvement too, as they don't 'mean' anything right now), and hide the text titles for each page. To make it more dynamic, perhaps it can change colour or create an outline around the page title when mouse-over, anything is possible. Create a plan to give it more of a wow-factor, while still being kinda elegant/minimalistic.

run.summary: Plan written:** `projects/mission-control/docs/sidebar-redesign-plan.md` What the plan covers: Collapse/Expand** Toggle button with rotating chevron Smooth 280ms `cubic-bezier` width animation Labels fade+slide before…

tasks.title: Futures bot improvement
tasks.description: Identify one safe improvement opportunity in the trading-bot workspace, such as diagnostics, reporting, review tooling, or non-execution reliability. When done identifying, create an implementation plan for this.

run.summary: Real improvement delivered: signal accuracy reporting script + implementation plan. Review note: task overshot the requested deliverable by implementing the improvement instead of only planning it; stale Alpaca-401 conclusion was later corrected.

feedback.note: Review outcome: promote to Done because the work is real and useful, but record that it did not fully follow the requested shape. The task was supposed to identify a safe improvement and produce an implementation plan; instead it implemented the improvement directly and only then wrote the plan. Keep the artifacts, but treat this as execution-overreach rather than a perfect task match.

tasks.title: Create a clean implementation plan for a collapsable top-section of the Chat page
tasks.description: The current top-section on the Chat page basically consists of two rows. The first holds things as the 'WS' status. 'Session' status, and the Context meter. The second holds things as the 'Seat', 'Model' and 'Recent sessions'. Create an implementation plan to collapse this second row, so more screen space available, whilst still showing that the status is live and the percentage of the context meter.

run.summary: Plan artifact:** `projects/mission-control/docs/chat-collapsible-top-section-plan.md` What the plan covers:** **Design**: Wrap Row 2 (Seat / Model / Effort / Recent Sessions) in a native `<details open>` element. Row…

tasks.title: Create a clean implementation plan for an improved Home page
tasks.description: The current Home page in Mission Control is basically still a placeholder, we want to make this more useful and appealing. Analyse this screenshot ('uploads/mission-control/Screenshot-03-24.jpg')  and use this as inspiration (but ignore the left side menu). It doesn't have to be an exact copy content-wise, but the design is desirable. Research and reason what widgets or elements would be useful to have on a Home page. Once done, create a plan to give it more of a wow-factor, while still being kinda elegant/minimalistic.

As mentioned previously, first  decide what is useful to have on Mission Control's Home page, then apply the design principles of the image (uploads/mission-control/Screenshot-03-24.jpg), then turn it into an actual implementation plan.
